:::::::::::::::::::: formula-card
### Trigonometry — Right-Angled Triangles {.unnumbered}

---

#### Labelling the Triangle {.unnumbered}

::: sticky-note-yellow
**💡 Key Rule** The labels **opposite**, **adjacent**, and **hypotenuse**
depend on which angle **θ** you are working from.
The **hypotenuse** is always opposite the right angle — it never changes.
:::

::: rule-group
**Three sides relative to angle θ**

```{=html}
<svg viewBox="0 0 280 180" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
     style="max-width:280px;width:100%;display:block;margin:10px auto">
  <!-- triangle: right angle bottom-left -->
  <polygon points="40,150 240,150 40,30" fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2.5"/>
  <!-- right-angle box -->
  <polyline points="40,135 55,135 55,150" fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1.8"/>
  <!-- angle arc at bottom-right -->
  <path d="M 210,150 A 30,30 0 0 0 207,126" fill="none" stroke="#555" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="195" y="140" font-size="14" font-style="italic" fill="#555">θ</text>
  <!-- side labels -->
  <text x="5"   y="95"  font-size="13" fill="#c0392b" font-weight="bold">Opposite</text>
  <text x="110" y="168" font-size="13" fill="#c0392b" font-weight="bold">Adjacent</text>
  <text x="130" y="85"  font-size="13" fill="#c0392b" font-weight="bold" transform="rotate(-35,155,85)">Hypotenuse</text>
</svg>
```

- **Hypotenuse (H)** — longest side, always opposite the right angle
- **Opposite (O)** — the side directly across from angle θ
- **Adjacent (A)** — the side next to θ (not the hypotenuse)
:::

---

#### SOH-CAH-TOA {.unnumbered}

::: sticky-note-blue
**🎯 Memory Trick** — **S**ome **O**ld **H**ouses **C**an **A**lways **H**ide **T**heir **O**ld **A**ge
:::

::: rule-group
**The three ratios**

$$\sin\theta = \frac{O}{H} \qquad \cos\theta = \frac{A}{H} \qquad \tan\theta = \frac{O}{A}$$

**Choosing the right ratio** — identify which two sides are involved (known + unknown), then pick the ratio that contains both of them.

| Known & Unknown | Ratio to use |
|---|---|
| Opp and Hyp | **sin** |
| Adj and Hyp | **cos** |
| Opp and Adj | **tan** |
:::

---

#### Finding a Missing Side {.unnumbered}

::: sticky-note-pink
**⚠️ Common Mistake** Check whether the unknown is on the **top** or **bottom** before rearranging. $x = H \times \sin\theta$ is very different from $x = \dfrac{O}{\sin\theta}$.
:::

::: rule-group
**Unknown on top** — multiply

$$\sin\theta = \frac{x}{H} \implies x = H \times \sin\theta$$

$$\cos\theta = \frac{x}{H} \implies x = H \times \cos\theta$$

$$\tan\theta = \frac{x}{A} \implies x = A \times \tan\theta$$

**Unknown on bottom** — divide

$$\sin\theta = \frac{O}{x} \implies x = \frac{O}{\sin\theta}$$

$$\cos\theta = \frac{A}{x} \implies x = \frac{A}{\cos\theta}$$

$$\tan\theta = \frac{O}{x} \implies x = \frac{O}{\tan\theta}$$

**Example** — find $x$ given hypotenuse = 10 cm, θ = 30°:
$$\sin 30° = \frac{x}{10} \implies x = 10 \times \sin 30° = 5 \text{ cm}$$
:::

---

#### Finding a Missing Angle {.unnumbered}

::: sticky-note-orange
**📝 Key Idea** Use the **inverse** trig function to undo the ratio.
On your calculator: press **SHIFT** (or **2nd**) then **sin / cos / tan**.
:::

::: rule-group
**The three inverse functions**

$$\theta = \sin^{-1}\!\left(\frac{O}{H}\right)$$

$$\theta = \cos^{-1}\!\left(\frac{A}{H}\right)$$

$$\theta = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{O}{A}\right)$$

**Example** — find θ given opposite = 5 cm, hypotenuse = 13 cm:
$$\sin\theta = \frac{5}{13} \implies \theta = \sin^{-1}\!\left(\frac{5}{13}\right) \approx 22.6°$$
:::

---

#### Angles of Elevation & Depression {.unnumbered}

::: sticky-note-yellow
**💡 Key Fact** Both angles are measured from the **horizontal**. They are **equal** (alternate angles) when the ground is level.
:::

::: rule-group
**Elevation** — angle measured *upward* from horizontal

```{=html}
<svg viewBox="0 0 300 140" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
     style="max-width:300px;width:100%;display:block;margin:10px auto">
  <!-- ground -->
  <line x1="20" y1="110" x2="280" y2="110" stroke="#888" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3"/>
  <!-- vertical tower -->
  <line x1="240" y1="110" x2="240" y2="25" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/>
  <!-- right angle -->
  <polyline points="240,95 225,95 225,110" fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <!-- line of sight -->
  <line x1="50" y1="110" x2="240" y2="25" stroke="#c0392b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <!-- elevation arc -->
  <path d="M 95,110 A 45,45 0 0 0 89,81" fill="none" stroke="#555" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="100" y="100" font-size="12" fill="#555" font-style="italic">θ</text>
  <!-- labels -->
  <text x="30"  y="125" font-size="11" fill="#555">Observer</text>
  <text x="245" y="70"  font-size="11" fill="#c0392b">h</text>
  <text x="125" y="105" font-size="11" fill="#c0392b">d</text>
</svg>
```

$$\tan\theta = \frac{h}{d} \qquad h = d\tan\theta \qquad d = \frac{h}{\tan\theta}$$

**Depression** — angle measured *downward* from horizontal

```{=html}
<svg viewBox="0 0 300 150" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
     style="max-width:300px;width:100%;display:block;margin:10px auto">
  <!-- cliff -->
  <rect x="30" y="30" width="30" height="100" fill="#ddd" stroke="#888" stroke-width="1"/>
  <!-- water -->
  <line x1="60" y1="130" x2="280" y2="130" stroke="#1d6fa4" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <!-- horizontal reference -->
  <line x1="60" y1="30" x2="260" y2="30" stroke="#888" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3"/>
  <!-- line of sight -->
  <line x1="60" y1="30" x2="240" y2="130" stroke="#c0392b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <!-- depression arc -->
  <path d="M 100,30 A 40,40 0 0 1 95,58" fill="none" stroke="#555" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="105" y="48" font-size="12" fill="#555" font-style="italic">θ</text>
  <!-- labels -->
  <text x="15"  y="80"  font-size="11" fill="#555">h</text>
  <text x="130" y="148" font-size="11" fill="#c0392b">d</text>
  <text x="220" y="128" font-size="11" fill="#555">Boat</text>
</svg>
```

$$\tan\theta = \frac{h}{d} \qquad \text{(same formula — alternate angles)}$$
:::

---

#### Angle Sum Reminder {.unnumbered}

::: rule-group
In any right-angled triangle the two acute angles sum to 90°:
$$\alpha + \beta = 90° \implies \beta = 90° - \alpha$$
:::

::::::::::::::::::::
